title: Delete the second engine; Strokes Gained is the only prescriptive source
reason: Two prescriptive engines created a black box to reverse-engineer; one rigorous engine beats two heuristic ones.
outcome: SG is the single source; every recommendation drills to its own raw shots.

title: Adopt Broadie scratch baseline over the PGA Tour baseline
reason: Scratch baseline makes SG magnitudes meaningful and cleans the category ranking.
outcome: Long-game cell magnitudes still marked VERIFY; ranking is robust regardless.

title: Add decision_quality, the one field SG cannot compute
reason: Splits losses into fix-by-thinking vs fix-by-practice; the only new field the data actually demanded.
outcome: Wired end to end; default Good, so 0% decisions is honest data not a bug.

title: Retire situation_created and short_sided
reason: SG does situation_created's job objectively, and short_sided was too subjective to mark reliably.
outcome: Columns dropped (migration 021); subtraction kept the input surface honest.

title: Gate every prescription on sample size, and show the n
reason: Honesty over overclaiming; below-threshold cuts never drive a recommendation.
outcome: clubs n>=15, buckets n>=10 govern eligibility everywhere.

title: No total_score column; always derive score from shots
reason: Never two sources of truth for one number; the cached score had drifted in the prototype.

title: Lock the name Round Recall
reason: Recall names both the mechanic (from memory) and the value (retrospective); ownable exact-match .com.
outcome: roundrecall.com is canonical; full repo rename still pending.

title: Reject Domino as the product name
reason: Owned by a pizza chain and a board game, unwinnable in search, and it explains a concept users only value after they get SG.
outcome: Kept as a metaphor in copy, not the name. It sells the room; Recall sells the door.

title: Elevate /rounds/[id] into a recall + takeaway view
reason: The missing half of the JTBD (turn a round into a story) and the best unbuilt screenshot for the splash.
outcome: Round now leads with a takeaway headline + per-hole SG ledger; editing preserved below.

title: Effort chip reads the real owner shot count, from memory
reason: A lived-in credibility signal grounded in real data, not a metrics block.
outcome: Uses getOwnerShotCount() (always V1_USER_ID), hides on 0 or error.

title: Reject coding-hours and tokens-used as effort signals
reason: Input and AI vanity; off-brand for a PM portfolio that should signal judgment, not volume.
outcome: Chose shots-logged-from-memory as the one understated signal instead.

title: Do not build a separate public demo or sample-data mode
reason: The sandbox (middleware cookie + seed_sandbox) plus the welcome overlay already do this; rebuilding is wasted effort.
outcome: A prior review's claim that hiring managers see nothing was wrong; verified against the code.

title: Keep momentum in exactly one dashboard section
reason: Restraint is the product decision; scattering trend arrows across screens is an explicit anti-goal.
outcome: One Momentum section + one compact in-table treatment; nothing else.

title: Defer the self-baseline blend until cells fill
reason: Seam is in place, but blending the player's own history in before cells are populated would be noise.
undeferWhen: per-cell sample reaches n>=30 (50% self), n>=75 (mostly self)

title: Defer offline entry to post-v1
reason: Out of scope for v1; not worth the sync complexity until the core analytics are proven.
undeferWhen: after core analytics are proven and on-course connectivity gaps prove painful

title: Build the first-domino root-cause read for blow-up holes
reason: The domino read is the differentiated insight no commercial app has, and it is testable now against my gut on existing data with no new capture.

title: What happened here? freeform reflection capture (MVP)
reason: I would not fill in a freeform text field regularly, and unstructured notes are low value. I prefer structured input and less typing. The why-layer should come back only as a structured, tap-based capture, not a text box.
undeferWhen: the first-domino read is frequently wrong on real holes AND the why-layer can be captured structurally (tap-based, extending decision_quality), never as freeform text

title: Finish the Round Recall rename across UI and metadata
reason: Splash, domain, and share card all say Round Recall but the tab title and install name still say Golf Tracker; a leftover label is a cheap credibility leak on the 30-to-60-second portfolio read.

title: Structured "why" chips on the named domino shot
reason: The existing 1-to-4 strike rating already captures the bad-swing case, and most domino shots are just bad swings, so the chip would largely re-collect data we have; the DL-017 gate (domino read proven wrong) also isn't met yet.
undeferWhen: the first-domino read is frequently wrong on live holes AND the why is a non-bad-swing texture the 1-to-4 strike rating + obstruction cannot already explain (e.g. a lie surprise like a ball coming out hot from fescue)

title: Turn your #1 leak into one practice game scored on the same baseline
reason: He has good practice habits but won't reliably log practice, and a personal leaderboard needs both repeatable pre-baked games and a felt need for it; today it risks being a solution in search of a problem.
undeferWhen: a small set of pre-baked, reliably-repeatable practice games exists that he plays regularly (e.g. 20 ten-footers, count makes), so a personal leaderboard has both data and a reason to exist; best tested first off-app with pen and paper

title: Ship the /thinking page (the PM-judgment artifact)
reason: Deferred alongside the practice idea with no specific objection raised; the standing case-against holds for now -- narration ranks below making the product itself more undeniable.
undeferWhen: he is actively sharing the live app with evaluators and wants the PM-judgment artifact ready to hand over

title: Practice-hole games: light SG-scored entry + personal leaderboard, in a new tab
reason: It's a routine he already does (90/70/50 practice balls); light entry plus a personal number-to-beat gives the diagnose-practice-verify loop real data, and it's walled off from real-round analytics so it can't distort the diagnosis.

title: Loosen the anti-badges stance: markers of real achievement are fine
reason: He doesn't hold a strong anti-badge view; badges are fine when they mark something true and earned (a real PR / mastery milestone), the way the Tiger 5 RECORD chip does. The standing line is about engagement-bait, not markers of honest achievement.

title: Call your leak: guess-before-reveal on the round recall view
reason: The guess-before-reveal interaction itself didn't land, but the engine mis-framed it: the calibration-streak / gamification element was written up as a liability in the case-against when Matt is actually drawn to earned streaks done right (DL-023, the Tiger 5 tracker). The streak instinct is a lead to pull forward, not a risk to steelman away.
undeferWhen: a gamification/earned-streak mechanic is designed in its own right (honest-achievement, not engagement-bait) and the guess-leak interaction can ride on top of it rather than carrying it

title: The Chase: turn the streak tracker from rear-view into a forward goal
reason: Lands the earned-streak instinct Matt corrected me on: reframes the existing rear-view streak section into a forward chase (closest-to-record target + 'X to go'), all from real golf already played. Honest-achievement, not engagement-bait; low effort since streaks.ts already has run + personal best; and a tasteful retention loop that doubles as a growth-PM portfolio signal.

title: Hayden Lake career: one earned-milestone ladder that consolidates the scattered records
reason: Pulls the scattered earned achievements (Tiger 5 streaks, course records, practice PRs, best-SG round) into one career surface a reflector wants to revisit, plus a 'not yet earned, how close' rung for forward pull. Every item tied to real golf, zero activity points (DL-023 line). Shipped despite my defer prediction; Matt's appetite for the gamification direction was stronger than I modeled.

title: Recent-form beat at the round-recall exit (finishes DL-025's unshipped half)
reason: Logging a round has real activation energy but the all-time SG numbers barely move (a cumulative-mean denominator problem). Added a single, priority-selected beat at the recall exit — a broken-record streak outranks a recent-form move outranks nothing — reading a shared pure primitive (recentForm.ts) plus recordsBrokenBy in streaks.ts. Both computed AS OF the round being viewed. Key judgment landed in live verification: the literal 'current run passed prior best' record rule fired on 12/19 rounds (Matt rarely doubles, so long streaks extend their own record nearly every round — wallpaper, not a beat). Redefined a record as a genuine comeback (a prior mark stood, broke, then got beaten), which cut it to a healthy 6 record / 8 move / 5 none across history.
outcome: Items 1+2 of the recent-form proposal shipped and DL-025 closed. Items 3 (dual number on the hero) and 4 (trajectory/ETA) stay in docs/pm-loop/recent-form-proposal.md for later. Live-verified across 19 rounds; 215 tests green.

title: Kill the trajectory/ETA read (recent-form proposal item 4)
reason: Off-app cheap test with real data killed it. Putting N=19, all-time mean M=-1.15/round, recent-3 rate r=-0.375 (a big swing). ETA to move the cumulative mean: ~26 rounds to -0.70, ~99 to -0.50, ~571 to -0.40, never to -0.30. Three problems: (1) glacial even at 3x better recent form, so an ETA confirms the number is stuck rather than making it feel alive; (2) asymptotic false precision (tiny target changes explode the horizon, all conditional on a 3-round rate holding for dozens of rounds) - exactly the overclaim the principles guard against; (3) the honest signal already shipped as the split-card strip ('picked up 0.62/round over your last 3 rounds'). Item 4 was flagged the most expendable of the four from the start.
undeferWhen: Never, unless the cumulative-mean denominator is replaced by a windowed/decaying baseline that actually moves - then a projection could be honest.
outcome: Recent-form workstream closes with items 1+2+3 shipped (DL-027 + the hero split card); item 4 killed by its own cheap test.
